false positive: RFC 8620 §5.2 defines maxChanges as UnsignedInt|null — null is a valid value meaning no limit

decision: build_request clones two short string literals per call; the allocation cost is negligible and adding indirection (Arc, OnceLock<Vec>) would complicate the code more than it saves
false positive: RFC 8620 §5.2 defines maxChanges as UnsignedInt|null; null is valid and means no limit; sending null is correct

decision: create-only methods take &CreateInput (always creating); space_invite_set takes Option<&CreateInput> because destroy-only calls are a real use case; inconsistency is intentional and follows spec semantics

decision: has_more_changes pagination pattern applies to all /changes responses via RFC 8620 §5.2; only space_invite_changes has distinct visibility rules worth calling out; adding boilerplate to every doc comment adds noise without value
decision: None::<&[&str]> at the call site is idiomatic for typed null in serde_json; consistent with spec (ids:null = all records)

Intentional: from_uri names the domain input (URI), as_str names the accessor convention. Rust std uses as_str() for str-view methods; from_uri() signals a domain-aware parse not a raw string parse. Asymmetry is justified. No code change.
